Intent:

revise: PRD is fundamentally sound and grounded but must not advance to TDD until the P1 ledger write-path gap is resolved
Require PRD to name who populates the terminal outcome at completion (worker-writes-ledger vs poll-backfill-only) and reconcile with P1
Require P3 to define the discrepancy equality basis (normalized comparison) to avoid false-positive discrepancy events
Require P2 backfill to flow through the P4 atomic completion helper to avoid contradicting P4
confidence.rationale: Decision is grounded in directly reading the worker launch path, poll logic, schema, and migration infra rather than relying on prose. The revise rationale (worker has no ledger handle) is verified in source. Residual uncertainty: a planned worker-side ledger write or a follow-on artifact may already intend to close the P1 gap, which I did not inspect (implementation-plan.md and tdd grill findings were not read in full).

payload.content: PRD is well-structured and grounded in current source (file-first poll marks missing-file jobs failed; migration/event infra exists). But the detached worker is a separate subprocess writing only result.json with no ledger handle, so the ledger can only capture the terminal outcome during poll. P1's promise that the result survives a missing result.json is unachievable for jobs deleted before their first poll. Decision: revise.

assumptions_to_verify: The atomic completion helper is invoked by poll (not the worker) under the current design
Discrepancy detection compares normalized outcomes, not raw bytes
Old result-file-only fixtures remain valid after migration adds nullable columns
contradictions_checked: P1 (durable regardless of file) vs Implementation Decisions (poll-centric, prefer ledger / file only when ledger empty) — tension confirmed
P2 backfill-on-poll vs P4 no-outcome-writes-outside-status-transaction — potential contradiction unless backfill uses the atomic helper
P5 scope fence vs a possible worker->ledger write being an S5 transport change — needs author ruling
missing_evidence: Whether implementation-plan.md already specifies a worker-side ledger write at completion
Whether the workflow CLI worker can open the same SQLite DB safely (WAL/locking) if given a write path
Whether the unpolled-then-deleted scenario is considered in-scope by the author
critical_review.strongest_objection: P1 promises the terminal result is readable from the ledger even if result.json is removed, but the detached worker writes only result.json and has no ledger handle, so a job completed and file-deleted before its first poll can never have its outcome durably captured, making P1 unachievable in that window.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the worker CLI already writes (or will write) the terminal outcome to the ledger at completion with safe cross-process SQLite access, or an explicit author scoping of P1 to 'survives file loss after first observation', would move this from revise to accept.

payload.content: PRD for making detached workflow terminal outcomes durable in the SQLite ledger is grounded against real source (poll_dual_agent_workflow_job, dual_agent_workflow_jobs, update_dual_agent_workflow_job all exist; current poll reads only result.json and fails on missing file, exactly the P1 gap). Contracts P1-P5 are coherent and map to real boundaries; all 5 grill findings resolved; issues decompose cleanly into 5 slices. Accept with one non-blocking clarification: P3/Slice5 do not define what 'differ' means for discrepancy detection, risking false-positive discrepancy events from serialization/redaction noise. That equality semantics must be pinned at the TDD gate.

critical_review.strongest_objection: P3/Slice5 do not define what it means for the ledger outcome and result.json cache to 'differ'. critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the discrepancy comparison is left as naive full-payload equality with no canonicalization and no downstream plan to constrain it would move this from accept to revise, because it would make the P3 promise produce misleading audit events in normal operation.

payload.intent: Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The 6-slice issues decomposition is grounded in real, current source artifacts
All 5 PRD promise contracts (P1-P5) and all 7 grill findings (F1-F7) have corresponding slices/ACs
The migration framework supports adding a forward migration #3 for terminal columns idempotently while preserving the S2 idempotency migration
The detached worker currently cannot locate its parent's ledger DB or its job_id, so Slice 3 is correctly the load-bearing slice
Slice 3 as written does not guarantee the worker and parent share the same ledger DB path

criteria: Source files cited exist and contain the referenced code (verified)
Every PRD promise and grill finding maps to at least one slice (verified by direct comparison)
Worker resolves ledger from config, not from a threaded parent db_path (verified workflow_cli.py:177, no --job-id arg)
Whether the TDD plan already requires a shared-db worker/poll test (NOT verified this gate)
evidence: REVISE: accept the 6-slice decomposition structure but require a revision before advancing

Slice 3 must add an acceptance criterion: detached worker writes to the same ledger DB the parent polls (db path threaded explicitly, not relying on --config default), proven by a worker-write then parent-poll test over one shared db_path
Coverage is otherwise complete: P1-P5 and grill findings F1-F7 all map to slices, so no structural redecomposition is needed
P3 delivered by a P1-priority Slice 6 is acceptable because Slice 4 already makes the ledger win structurally; Slice 6 adds only the discrepancy event/observability
confidence.rationale: All factual claims about the decomposition and the worker DB-path gap are verified against current source with file:line evidence, so the strongest objection is well-founded. Confidence is held below 0.9 because the downstream TDD plan (a separate artifact I did not deeply read here) may already encode the worker/parent shared-db requirement, which would downgrade the objection from must-fix to already-handled; and because no tests were run for an issues-review gate.

payload.content: Issues breakdown for S3a is well-grounded: all 6 slices map to real source artifacts, and all 5 PRD promises plus 7 grill findings are covered. One load-bearing gap: Slice 3 threads job_id but never requires the detached worker to write to the SAME ledger DB the parent polls; the worker resolves db from --config default while the parent uses its own State db_path, silently breaking P1 under test and non-default deployments. Decision: revise (add a Slice 3 acceptance criterion for worker/parent ledger-path identity); otherwise accept-grade.

assumptions_to_verify: The TDD/implementation will thread the parent's actual db_path (or a config that resolves to it) into the worker, not just job_id
result.json file outcome is redacted with the same canonicalization as the ledger before discrepancy comparison (Slice 6 AC)
cfg.supervisor.state_db resolves to the parent ledger in all real submit paths
contradictions_checked: Slice 4 AC 'do not mark missing file as failure for completed jobs' vs existing worker_exited_without_result path (stdio.py:1942) - consistent: that path requires no ledger outcome, no file, and dead pid, which ledger-first poll bypasses for completed jobs
Slice 1 'keeps S2 idempotency columns/indexes' vs migration framework - consistent: migration #2 is preserved and #3 would be additive
P3 marked P1 priority vs being a named PRD promise - consistent: Slice 4 already makes the ledger authoritative; Slice 6 adds only observability
missing_evidence: Whether the TDD plan (tdd.md) already mandates a worker-write then parent-poll assertion over one shared db_path
Whether cfg.supervisor.state_db is guaranteed equal to the parent State db_path in production submit paths
Whether tests construct the parent State from the same config the worker receives or from an unrelated temp DB
critical_review.strongest_objection: Slice 3 threads job_id but never requires the detached worker to persist its terminal outcome into the SAME ledger DB the parent polls; the worker derives its DB from cfg.supervisor.state_db via --config (default ~/.codex-supervisor/config.yaml) while the parent reads self.state.db_path, so in tests and non-default deployments the worker-side write (P1) lands in a different database than the ledger-first poll (Slice 4) reads, silently defeating the feature's headline promise.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If tdd.md already requires the worker-side terminal ledger write to be proven against the SAME db_path the parent polls (a shared-db fixture), the objection downgrades to already-handled and the gate becomes a clean accept; conversely, if the worker DB path cannot be made to match the parent's, the gate should move toward deny pending a Slice 3 scope rework.

Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. payload.content: Reviewed the S3a TDD plan against live source. All 5 PRD promises and 6 slices have mapped tests; RED-state claims verified accurate against current file-based poll, status-only update helper, and S2-only migration. Two revise-level gaps: missing negative discrepancy test (match -> no spurious event) and atomicity proven by construction rather than tested. Recommend revise with bounded additions.

assumptions_to_verify: The new completion helper will be a single UPDATE statement so atomicity is SQLite-guaranteed
Backfill on legacy poll routes through the same atomic helper and is idempotent on repeat polls
Ledger-first read occurs before the dead-pid failure branch for terminal jobs
contradictions_checked: Whether complete_dual_agent_workflow_job already exists (it does not; correctly new)
Whether poll already reads ledger (it does not; file-only, RED valid)
Whether terminal columns already exist (they do not; migration RED valid)
Whether result.json is redacted vs raw relative to ledger (PRD specifies redacting both sides before compare; coherent)
missing_evidence: A negative discrepancy test proving matching ledger+cache emits no event
A failure-injection or single-statement assertion proving terminal status+outcome cannot be partially committed
Explicit assertion that test 6 migration preserves S2 idempotency columns/index (currently leaned on regression suite)
Confirmation the worker CLI completion path currently writes result.json without any ledger write (inferred from submit path, not directly read)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The discrepancy test (test 4) only asserts the positive case (ledger!=cache -> event). It never asserts the negative case (ledger==cache -> no spurious event), which is precisely the false-positive failure mode Finding 7 and P3's canonicalization contract were written to prevent. After test 3's backfill, a repeat poll has matching ledger and cache and must stay clean, but nothing covers that.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the plan added a match->no-discrepancy test and stated the single-statement atomicity rationale (or a partial-commit guard test), I would move from revise to accept.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

All TDD acceptance tests map to existing code seams

Residual: column naming unspecified; no tests executed

job_id threading after reservation does not perturb S2 idempotency (already in exclusion set at stdio.py:109)

payload.content:
Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: No source or test files are modified in the working tree; only docs/ is untracked.
Threading job_id into request.json does not alter S2 idempotency dedup.
A single BEGIN IMMEDIATE state helper can atomically write terminal status, terminal columns, and a terminal event with shared rollback.
Two existing migration-list assertions will require a v3 entry once migration 3 is added.
Discrepancy comparison must redact both ledger and cache before sorted-key comparison to avoid false positives.

Read current source for state.py, schema_migrations.py, codex_supervisor_stdio.py, codex_supervisor_workflow_cli.py, redaction.py and relevant tests
Confirmed absence of implementation diffs via git status
evidence: Gate decision: REVISE — execution plan is correct and implementable against current source, but the execution artifact (code+test diffs) is absent and could not be produced (writes blocked); generate the diffs and run the focused+full suites, then re-review.
lead specialist decision: revise — sound plan, missing artifact.
confidence.rationale: High confidence in the review itself: I read all six planning artifacts and the current source for every target module plus the event/redaction helpers, and traced the migration, idempotency, and poll interactions for contradictions, finding none fundamental. Confidence is capped below 0.9 because no implementation was executed or test-verified, and the implement-vs-review intent of the gate is ambiguous.

payload.content: Reviewed the S3a execution gate against current source. The plan is sound and implementable: atomic single-transaction completion is feasible via _insert_event_unlocked, job_id threading does not affect S2 dedup, ledger-first poll with legacy fallback is consistent, and discrepancy canonicalization is coherent if both sides are redacted. However, no implementation artifact exists (git shows only untracked docs) and file writes were not permitted in this session, so the execution gate cannot be marked accepted. Recommend REVISE: produce diffs+tests under TDD, then re-review.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.

Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Intent:
Make the detached workflow job's terminal outcome durable in the SQLite ledger so reconnecting clients can poll the final result from the ledger even when result.json is missing. Scope: add terminal outcome fields/migration to dual_agent_workflow_jobs; write terminal status and outcome atomically; make poll_dual_agent_workflow_job read ledger first; keep writing and reading result.json as a legacy cache only when the ledger lacks an outcome; record a discrepancy event when cache and ledger differ, with ledger winning. Non-goals: S3b event sourcing, S1/S2/S5 transport changes, gate/reviewer semantic changes.
